title: Leucanthemum subalpinum
intro: Leucanthemum subalpinum, also known as Alpine Daisy, is a perennial flowering plant native to the mountainous regions of Europe. The health benefits of Leucanthemum subalpinum include its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in treating wounds, reducing fever, and fighting off infections. The bioactive constituents of this plant include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Leucanthemum subalpinum include tea, tincture, and infusion, which can be used to treat a variety of ailments, including digestive issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Leucanthemum subalpinum is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. This plant is a member of the genus Leucanthemum, which is a group of daisy-like plants. Leucanthemum subalpinum is further classified as a perennial herb, and its classification is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Asterales, family - Asteraceae, genus - Leucanthemum, species - L. subalpinum. The classification of Leucanthemum subalpinum is also sometimes referred to as a type of mountain daisy due to its alpine habitat. This classification is used to group and identify the plant among other species.
uses: Leucanthemum subalpinum, also known as alpine daisy, has been used in traditional medicine due to its potential antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's flowers and leaves are said to have been used to treat various health issues, including digestive problems and skin conditions. Some studies suggest that the plant's extracts may have antimicrobial and antiseptic properties, which could aid in wound healing. However, further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness and safety of using Leucanthemum subalpinum for medicinal purposes. More studies are required to fully understand its potential health benefits and applications.
